title: Fix category page infinite scroll by extracting useCategoryProductsInfinite hook

requirements.text: Create a new custom hook `useCategoryProductsInfinite` in `frontend/src/hooks/useCategoryProductsInfinite.ts` that encapsulates all pagination logic for category products. The hook must: accept `categoryId` (string) and `pageSize` (number) parameters; internally manage accumulated products array, current page, initial loading state, load-more loading state, hasMore flag, and totalCount; expose `{ products, isLoading, isLoadingMore, error, hasMore, loadMore, totalCount }`; use `useCategoryProductsPaginated` as the underlying data fetcher; prevent duplicate fetches with an in-flight guard; reset all state when `categoryId` or `pageSize` changes; and clean up on unmount. Mirror the architectural pattern of `useHomepageCategoriesInfinite`.

quotes: Create a new hook `useCategoryProductsInfinite` that mimics the home page pattern
Clean up on unmount or category change

acceptanceCriteria: Hook returns `{ products, isLoading, isLoadingMore, error, hasMore, loadMore, totalCount }`
Calling `loadMore` appends next page products to the accumulated array without clearing existing ones
Changing `categoryId` resets accumulated products and page to initial state
Duplicate fetches are prevented when a fetch is already in flight
Hook cleans up timers/state on unmount

requirements.text: Refactor `frontend/src/pages/public/CategoryPage.tsx` to use the new `useCategoryProductsInfinite` hook and remove all manual pagination state management. The component must: remove `page`, `accumulatedProducts`, `useEffect`-based accumulation, and timer-based logic; use `useIsMobile` to set `pageSize` (5 mobile / 10 desktop); use `useInfiniteScroll` with a throttled scroll handler (500px threshold) and a sentinel ref via `useIntersectionObserver`; render the product grid at all times when products exist; render the load-more spinner separately below the grid (never replace the grid); show an initial full-screen spinner only on first load; show end-of-results message when `hasMore` is false and products exist; preserve all Spanish UI text; and not modify `index.css`.

quotes: Simplify the component to match home page structure
Products grid always visible when products exist
Loading spinner rendered separately below grid
No complex timer logic mixing with data fetching
Single source of truth for products array

acceptanceCriteria: CategoryPage no longer contains `accumulatedProducts` state, manual `page` state, or `useEffect`-based accumulation logic
Initial load shows a centered spinner; product grid renders once products are available
Scrolling to the bottom triggers `loadMore` and shows a spinner below the existing product grid without hiding it
Previously loaded products remain fully visible during a paginated fetch
New products append below existing products without any flicker or grid reset
Spinner disappears after the paginated fetch completes
End-of-results message appears in Spanish when all products are loaded
Desktop loads 10 products per page; mobile loads 5 products per page

constraints: Do not modify any file under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
All Spanish-language UI strings must be preserved as-is
nonGoals: Changes to the home page or HomepageCategoriesSection
Changes to the backend pagination API
Adding new UI features beyond the infinite scroll fix
